when_to_help: Student confuses SI and CI formulas
Difficulty determining which interest type to use
Struggling with compound interest exponent calculations

key_concepts.explanation: SI = P × R × T / 100. Interest is calculated only on the original principal every year — linear growth.
A = P(1 + R/100)^T. Interest is calculated on the accumulated amount each period — exponential growth. Always find Amount first, then CI = Amount - Principal.
For exactly 2 years: Difference = P × (R/100)². CI always exceeds SI (for R > 0, T > 1 year).

pro_tips: SI is simpler but always smaller than CI for same P, R, T
For compounding N times per year: use (R/N) for rate and (N×T) for time
Always write down P, R, T clearly before choosing a formula
